source.title: Predictive coding models for pain perception

confidence_rationale: Most REE-relevant paper in the pull. Builds a biophysical predictive-coding model with S1 (sensory-discriminative) and ACC (affective-emotional) as two coupled but distinct populations, fit to rat LFP data from both regions. This is essentially the computational architecture REE is proposing with E2_harm_s (SD-032b's upstream sensory predictor) and E2_harm_a (upstream affective predictor) as two coupled populations. Direct empirical + computational grounding.
failure_signatures: A single unified pain forward model is falsified: S1 and ACC populations have distinct temporal dynamics and require separate model variables.
A fully decoupled parallel model is also weakened: the paper models S1 and ACC with explicit coupling terms, not as independent populations.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Pain is a multidimensional experience involving sensory-discriminative (S1) and affective-emotional (ACC) processes. A predictive-coding model with coupled but distinct S1 and ACC populations reproduces LFP dynamics in both naive and chronic-pain animals, including expectation effects, placebo/nocebo, and chronic-pain alterations.
mapping.ree_translation: Direct biological and computational precedent for REE's architecture. S1 and ACC carry distinct forward-prediction populations (-> z_harm_s and z_harm_a in REE) but are coupled at the network level (-> shared trunk or crosstalk between E2_harm_s and E2_harm_a). The paper's coupling parameters are the computational analog of what REE would call shared-substrate weights.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Rat LFP data (not human) and a population-level model (not a neural network forward model). The coupling between S1 and ACC in the model is phenomenological, not cast as a literal shared latent trunk.
mapping.source_context: Rodent electrophysiology + computational model, naive and chronic-pain conditions.

confidence_components.notes: Transfer risk elevated because rat LFP to REE computational agent is a long mapping path. Mapping fidelity is very high because the architecture directly parallels ours.
